channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
item.description: The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration to force foreigners in the U.S. to apply for a green card abroad
item.description: Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.
item.content:encoded:
Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.

(Image credit: Wilfredo Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 1 person died, 36 injured after blast at New York City shipyard, officials say
item.description: One person has died after a blast Friday at a New York City shipyard, officials say. They said 36 people were injured, most of them firefighters and other first responders.
item.content:encoded:
One person has died after a blast Friday at a New York City shipyard, officials say. They said 36 people were injured, most of them firefighters and other first responders.

(Image credit: Kevin S. Vineys)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 40,000 people under evacuation orders after chemical tank leak in Southern California
item.description: About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical that officials said could rupture or explode.
item.content:encoded:
About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical that officials said could rupture or explode.

(Image credit: Jeff Gritchen/The Orange County Register)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal judge dismisses criminal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: U.S. District Judge Waverly Crenshaw accused the Justice Department of conducting a vindictive prosecution against the Salvadoran man.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. District Judge Waverly Crenshaw accused the Justice Department of conducting a vindictive prosecution against the Salvadoran man.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: How a spirit of kindness grew in the aftermath of a tornado — and remains 15 years on
item.description: Fifteen years ago, after a massive tornado flattened much of Joplin, Mo., nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild. A spirit of community service lives on in Joplin today.
item.content:encoded: Fifteen years ago, after a massive tornado flattened much of Joplin, Mo., nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild. A spirit of community service lives on in Joplin today.
item.dc:creator: Pauline Bartolone

item.title: DHS says ICE has 'no relationship' with spyware maker Paragon Solutions
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security says ICE has no current contract or relationship with the company, but questions remain about ICE's use of commercial spyware.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Homeland Security says ICE has no current contract or relationship with the company, but questions remain about ICE's use of commercial spyware.

(Image credit: Timothy A. Clary)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Gabbard resigns as national intelligence director citing husband's cancer diagnosis
item.description: Gabbard is the latest in a series of Cabinet officials to leave the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
Gabbard is the latest in a series of Cabinet officials to leave the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? The best options for saving gas
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?
item.content:encoded:
The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Commission of Fine Arts gives Trump's arch final approval, despite public pushback
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts has given a design for a massive arch in Washington, D.C., its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components.
item.content:encoded: The Commission of Fine Arts has given a design for a massive arch in Washington, D.C., its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
